items.title: Pokies changes will 'take Tasmania backwards', gaming commission says

children.content: The body tasked with implementing Tasmania's new pokies policies says they won't work to reduce harm to problem gamblers and could make things worse. The Liquor and Gaming Commission was not consulted on the changes, which include ATMs in gaming venues.

gallery.altText: A man sitting in front of a poker machine. Two more poker machines are next to him

thumbnail.altText: A man sitting in front of a poker machine. Two more poker machines are next to him

items.title: What happened when a rare bird found a place to nest near a logging coupe

children.content: It was late 2021 when one of Australia's rarest birds started to arrive in the native forests of Lonnavale. Ecologists had expected it but for a reacting forestry sector, it was "not sustainable".

image.altText: A fledgling swift parrot pokes its head out of a tree hollow.

items.title: Former National Trust director unfairly dismissed, Fair Work Commission finds

children.content: The Fair Work Commission has found Scott Carlin, former managing director of the National Trust of Tasmania, was unfairly dismissed just two months before his two-year contract was set to expire.

image.altText: A man wearing a hat smiles at the camera.

items.title: Hogtied robbery victim had to 'watch his house burn down'

children.content: A victim of an early morning robbery was hogtied and had to watch for hours as his home burnt to the ground after two home invaders robbed him and then set fire to his property, his family says.

image.altText: An older man with two football players beside him.

items.title: When Sam Neill came to Tasmania

children.content: As the world remembers actor Sam Neill, former film festival curator Owen Tilbury remembers when he convinced Sam Neill to come to Tasmania.

items.title: Developers or renters? The real winners out of affordable housing schemes

children.content: State and federal governments are offering incentives for property developers in exchange for more affordable housing. But a Four Corners investigation has uncovered major flaws in the strategy.

items.title: Exposing illicit tobacco's online marketplace

children.content: The process was simple and the service was speedy but the ease with which 7.30 acquired illicit tobacco products was concerning.

image.altText: Cigarettes on a table.

items.title: Self-managed super funds could have to pay compensation

children.content: The assistant treasurer is looking to revamp funding for the Compensation Scheme of Last Resort, saying that large super funds, as well as people with self-managed super funds (SMSFs), could be asked to chip in.

image.altText: A woman who lost her super sits at home with a sad face

items.title: 'Kill him': Muslim man attacked on way to mosque

children.content: A Muslim man has escaped a near-miss knife attack on his way to evening prayers after police say he was set upon by two men metres away from his local mosque.

image.altText: A photo of Samir in the hospital. His lip and eyes are bleeding and bruised.

items.title: Trump vows to take over Strait of Hormuz and says US will impose fees

children.content: Donald Trump says the US is reinstating its blockade of Iran in the Strait of Hormuz and will start charging for ships from elsewhere to transit through it.

image.altText: A ship out on the sea.

items.title: 'Jurassic family': Spielberg leads outpouring of tributes for actor Sam Neill

children.content: The New Zealand actor died suddenly at the age of 78, with Hollywood directors and actors and prominent political figures all paying tribute to a great of global cinema.

items.title: Jewish professor says 'intimidating' protest changed his life on campus

children.content: Melbourne University professor Steven Prawer hopes the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion will lead to campus change, following a 2024 demonstration that saw protesters invade his office.

image.altText: Man in blue shirt stands in front of office window and indoor plant

items.title: Some women seeking sperm donation are being coerced into sex

children.content: Australia is facing a sperm donation shortage and it is leading those desperate to find a way to conceive down some expensive and potentially dangerous paths.

image.altText: A woman's pregnant belly.

items.title: Yemen's Houthis target Saudi airport with ballistic missiles and drones

children.content: The Iran-aligned Houthi movement that controls northern Yemen has accused Saudi Arabia of launching air strikes against the international airport in Sanaa.

image.altText: A smoke cloud seen above buildings.

items.title: UK counter-terrorism police now leading Widdecombe murder investigation

children.content: The counter-terrorism unit has taken charge of the case due to "new information and evidence" on the suspected murder of the former minister, the UK Home Secretary says.

image.altText: An older lady speaks to the media.

items.title: Man who sneaked into Australia 45 years ago has lived in limbo until now

children.content: Kwong Wan Lo stowed away on a ship bound for Australia 45 years ago. With no legal right to stay, he built a life in Sydney anyway.

gallery.altText: A portrait of Kwong Wan Lo wearing a black jacket.

items.title: Sea World inquest hears crash could have been avoided if pilot looked right

children.content: The chief executive of Sea World Helicopters told a Brisbane inquest he believed pilot Ashley Jenkinson was "totally distracted" by the front row passengers on the joy flight.

image.altText: Two bystanders look on at helicopter crash scene.

items.title: Name revealed of Sydney daycare worker facing 329 alleged child abuse charges

children.content: A Sydney childcare worker faces more than 320 charges over his alleged abuse of 136 children over a 16-year period, as police reveal a list of locations where he worked.

items.title: How a young doctor ended up being charged with manslaughter after street scuffle

children.content: A young Perth doctor and musician is behind bars, accused of killing a 63-year-old man after a late-night Fremantle scuffle. What do we know?

children.content: The Queensland opposition has labelled the cuts to family and domestic violence funding "indefensible".

items.title: What Elon Musk is getting wrong about The Odyssey

children.content: The tech trillionaire has spent a good chunk of 2026 yelling on X about how Christopher Nolan's adaptation of Homer's Odyssey "desecrates" the ancient poet.

items.title: 'Devoted' Melbourne mother remembered by family after stabbing death

children.content: Lavanya Chappa has been remembered as a devoted mother and dear friend, one week after the mother-of-two was stabbed to death in her Melbourne home.

items.title: How Lindsey Graham's death could affect Donald Trump's agenda

children.content: US senator Lindsey Graham died suddenly on Saturday night, local time, creating an unexpected vacancy in Congress. Here's what we know about what comes next.

items.title: Australian woman was cunning 'Sharia judge' in Syrian camp, ABC told

children.content: Speaking exclusively to the ABC, a security source who dealt directly with Australian woman Hodan Abby calls her a driving force behind many of a Syrian detention camp's problems.

items.title: 'He continues to inspire us': Richard Scolyer's family pay emotional tribute

children.content: Thousands have gathered for Richard Scolyer's state memorial service, with the leading melanoma pathologist remembered as an esteemed researcher and family man.

articles.title: Tour de Cure reflects on Scolyer's legacy ahead of memorial service

children.content: As Mark Beretta rode his bike up a steep Tasmanian mountain in March with all his might, it was none other than Richard Scolyer who overtook him.

items.title: Coroner calls for change after man died while paramedic waited outside

children.content: A paramedic who chose not to force entry to a house, despite being told a man was inside and needed medical attention, had "a flawed understanding" of his powers to enter the property, an inquest finds.

image.altText: A green and yellow ambulance driving on a road

items.title: 'Psychopath' Tuchel gives England a chance to prove its mettle

children.content: This England World Cup squad is proving they are different to their predecessors at every stop. Now their manager has just given them their latest challenge.

items.title: Bazball: An uncomplicated idea leaving a very complex legacy

children.content: Brendon McCullum deliberately set out to make England's men's Test team a less complicated team to play cricket for. After four years of Bazball's highs and lows, it leaves a very complicated legacy.

items.title: Sentencing of Molly Ticehurst's killer brought forward

children.content: The sentencing of the man who murdered New South Wales woman Molly Ticehurst has been brought forward to allow a psychiatrist to give evidence.
